items.title: Validation error vs model complexity (U-curve)
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  low["Low complexity"]
  mid["Moderate complexity"]
  high["High complexity"]
  hb["High bias / underfitting"]
  sweet["Validation error minimum"]
  hv["High variance / overfitting"]
  low --> hb
  mid --> sweet
  high --> hv
items.caption: Validation error typically falls then rises as complexity increases — underfitting on the left, overfitting on the right, sweet spot in the middle.

items.title: Complexity knob moves along bias–variance tradeoff
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  knob["Increase model complexity"]
  lb["Lower bias"]
  hv["Higher variance"]
  knob2["Decrease model complexity"]
  hb["Higher bias"]
  lv["Lower variance"]
  knob --> lb --> hv
  knob2 --> hb --> lv
items.caption: Turning the capacity dial trades bias for variance; Bias and Variance topics define those error terms — this topic owns the dial.

items.title: Tuning workflow from baseline to sweet spot
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  base["Simple baseline model"]
  sweep["Sweep complexity hyperparameter"]
  plot["Plot train vs validation error"]
  pick["Pick simplest near val minimum"]
  base --> sweep --> plot --> pick
items.caption: Start simple, increase complexity along one axis, and stop at the least complex model with validation error near its minimum.
